### The ‚ÄúSudden panic‚Äù trend

This trend is all about how onetinymoment can instantly spiral into full chaos. Creators take something completely normal and turn it into an exaggerated emotional reaction.
The setup is simple: a small trigger ‚Üí a big overreaction.
How to do the trend
‚Ä¢ Start with a normal, everyday situation
‚Ä¢ Add text overlay explaining the tiny trigger
‚Ä¢ Show your reaction escalating quickly + increase the video speed to show the rising anxiety
Scenario examples:
Weave in the product as the solution to the anxiety as you calm down towards the end.
‚Ä¢ Finance app: trigger: checking bank accounttext: ‚Äúwhen i check my balance once and start rethinking every decision i‚Äôve made‚Äù
‚Ä¢ Study app: trigger: one assignmenttext: ‚Äúwhen i see one deadline and realize i have 5 more i ignored‚Äù

### The ‚ÄúNo you don‚Äôt get to ask me questions‚Äù trend

This trend is built around playful defensiveness. Using the line‚Äúno, you don‚Äôt get to ask questions,‚Äùcreators show moments where they‚Äôre clearly doing something questionable‚Ä¶ and refuse to explain it.
The humor comes from the tension: something doesn‚Äôt add up, but instead of clarifying, they shut it down completely.
It‚Äôs simple, slightly chaotic, and very relatable.
How to do the trend
‚Ä¢ Use the ‚Äúno, you don‚Äôt get to ask questions‚Äù audio
‚Ä¢ Film yourself reacting or lip syncing
‚Ä¢ Add text overlay to set the situation
‚Ä¢ Keep the context slightly confusing (or disappointed when using for ads)
How can creators use it:
Use the sound as a backdrop while showing how life feels easier, calmer, or more enjoyable thanks to the product or app.
‚Ä¢ Business: when they‚Äôre still doing everything manually after i showed them the app that automates it
‚Ä¢ Dating: when they complain about bad matches but refuse to fix their profile using the app

### The ‚ÄúAnd Emily....that's all‚Äù trend

This trend brings back pureMiranda Priestly energy. Using the iconic ‚Äúand Emily‚Ä¶ that‚Äôs all‚Äù line, creators create a contrast between two people, two versions of themselves, or two ways of doing something.
One side is polished, put-together, or ‚Äúcorrect.‚Äù The other is‚Ä¶ not.
It's all about the subtle judgment.
How to do the trend
‚Ä¢ Use the ‚Äúand Emily‚Ä¶ that‚Äôs all‚Äù audioFilm two contrasting shots: polished vs chaotic or doing it right vs doing it wrong
‚Ä¢ Film two contrasting shots
‚Ä¢ Keep expressions minimal and slightly dismissive
Scenario examples:
Turn it into ajudgment format, especially around doing somethingwithoutthe product vswithit.
‚Ä¢ Productivity: you organized, calm, everything in one place vs someone using 5 different tools, messy tabs everywhere
